exams.name: IBPS PO Mains 2026
exams.desc: New pattern — Essay & Comprehension (25 marks, Letter Writing removed)

questions.text: You are a PO at SBI. A customer Mr. Ramesh Gupta has complained that his net banking access has been blocked for 3 days. Write a formal email explaining the reason (scheduled maintenance) and the resolution timeline.
questions.modelAnswer:
To: ramesh.gupta@email.com
Subject: Resolution of Net Banking Access Issue

Dear Mr. Gupta,

I sincerely apologise for the inconvenience caused due to the disruption in your net banking services.

The temporary suspension was due to scheduled server maintenance from 14th to 16th June 2026. Your net banking access has been restored. Please reset your password if you face any difficulty.

For assistance, contact 1800-11-2211.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: You are a PO at a semi-urban SBI branch. An elderly woman, Mrs. Kamla Devi (72), reports that she shared her OTP with someone posing as SBI customer care and ₹45,000 was debited. She is in tears. Describe the steps you will take immediately.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would first calm Mrs. Kamla Devi and offer her a seat. My immediate priority would be to block her debit card through the bank system and help her call 1800-11-2211 to report the fraud.

I would assist her in filing a written complaint at the branch with all details of the transaction. I would also guide her to file a cybercrime complaint at cybercrime.gov.in.

I would explain the RBI Zero Liability policy, which protects customers from unauthorised transactions if reported within three working days. Finally, I would educate her about never sharing OTPs with anyone, including bank staff.

questions.text: You recently participated in a Financial Literacy Camp in the village of Rampur on 5th June 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager covering objectives, activities, attendance, outcomes and recommendations.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON FINANCIAL LITERACY CAMP — RAMPUR VILLAGE

Date: 6th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Financial Literacy Camp — 5th June 2026

A Financial Literacy Camp was organised at Gram Panchayat Hall, Rampur on 5th June 2026. The camp covered PMJDY accounts, RuPay card benefits, UPI usage, and banking fraud awareness.

180 villagers attended including 110 women. 42 new PMJDY accounts were opened and 8 RuPay cards activated.

Recommendation: Monthly camps should be organised targeting remaining hamlets and a dedicated Business Correspondent should be deployed.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in ~100 words.

"Financial inclusion remains one of India's most pressing challenges. Despite PMJDY bringing millions into formal banking, merely opening accounts is not enough. Active usage for credit, savings, insurance and remittances is the real measure of inclusion. Financial literacy, digital infrastructure, and trust in banking institutions must be strengthened simultaneously. Without addressing these interconnected challenges, universal financial inclusion will remain elusive."
questions.modelAnswer: Financial inclusion remains a major challenge in India. While PMJDY has brought millions into the banking system, account ownership alone is insufficient. True inclusion means active usage for savings, credit, insurance, and transfers. Achieving this requires improving financial literacy, expanding digital infrastructure, and building trust in banks. Unless these challenges are addressed together, universal financial inclusion will remain difficult to achieve.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250–300 words on the following topic:

"Digital Banking and Financial Inclusion: Opportunities and Challenges in India."
questions.modelAnswer:
India has witnessed a remarkable shift towards digital banking over the past decade, driven by initiatives such as the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), and the spread of affordable smartphones and internet connectivity. This transformation has opened up unprecedented opportunities to extend formal financial services to previously unbanked populations, particularly in rural and semi-urban areas.

UPI alone processes billions of transactions every month, allowing even small vendors and farmers to receive payments instantly without visiting a branch. Digital lending platforms have made credit accessible to small businesses lacking traditional collateral, while mobile-based insurance and investment products have widened the reach of financial planning to first-time users.

However, several challenges limit the full potential of this shift. Digital literacy remains uneven, with many elderly and rural users struggling to navigate banking apps confidently. Cybersecurity threats, including phishing and OTP fraud, disproportionately affect first-time digital users unfamiliar with safe practices. Connectivity gaps in remote regions further continue to exclude a significant section of the population.

To address this, banks must invest in vernacular-language interfaces, simplified user experiences, and continuous financial literacy campaigns run with local self-help groups and Business Correspondents. Strengthening cybersecurity awareness and grievance redressal is equally important to build trust.

In conclusion, while digital banking has significantly advanced financial inclusion in India, true inclusion will only be achieved when access is matched by usage, trust and safety — a balance of technology, education and protection.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) has emerged as one of the most successful digital payment innovations in the world, processing billions of transactions every month across India. Launched in 2016 by the National Payments Corporation of India, UPI allows users to transfer money instantly between bank accounts using just a mobile number or a virtual payment address, without sharing sensitive banking details. Its real-time nature, zero transaction cost for small payments, and interoperability across banks and apps have made it the preferred mode of payment for millions of Indians, from large retailers to roadside vendors. The success of UPI has drawn international attention, with several countries exploring similar real-time payment systems or partnering with India to enable UPI-based transactions for tourists and businesses abroad. However, experts caution that the rapid growth of digital payments must be matched by stronger fraud prevention measures, as cybercriminals increasingly target inexperienced users through fake payment requests and phishing links."

(a) What does UPI allow users to do, and what information is required to make a transfer? (2 marks)
(b) Name two factors that have made UPI the preferred mode of payment in India. (2 marks)
(c) How has UPI's success been recognised beyond India's borders? (2 marks)
(d) What concern do experts raise regarding the rapid growth of digital payments? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage that means "the quality of being able to work together across different systems." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) UPI allows users to transfer money instantly between bank accounts using only a mobile number or virtual payment address, without sharing sensitive banking details.
(b) Its real-time nature and zero transaction cost for small payments, along with interoperability across banks and apps, have made it the preferred payment mode.
(c) Several countries are exploring similar real-time payment systems or partnering with India to enable UPI-based transactions for tourists and businesses abroad.
(d) Experts caution that growth must be matched by stronger fraud prevention measures, as cybercriminals target inexperienced users through fake payment requests and phishing links.
(e) Interoperability.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250–300 words on the following topic:

"Artificial Intelligence in Banking: Transforming Customer Service and Risk Management."
questions.modelAnswer:
Artificial Intelligence is rapidly reshaping the banking sector, fundamentally altering how financial institutions interact with customers and manage risk. From chatbots handling routine queries around the clock to algorithms detecting fraudulent transactions in real time, AI has become central to modern banking operations.

In customer service, AI-powered virtual assistants now resolve a large share of routine queries instantly, freeing human staff to focus on complex grievances and relationship management. Personalised product recommendations based on a customer's transaction history have improved cross-selling while enhancing satisfaction. Voice and chat interfaces in regional languages are also making banking more accessible to non-English speaking customers.

On the risk management front, AI models analyse vast volumes of transaction data to identify unusual patterns indicative of fraud, often flagging suspicious activity within seconds — far faster than traditional rule-based systems. Credit scoring has similarly benefited, with machine learning models incorporating alternative data such as utility payments to assess creditworthiness for customers without a formal credit history.

Despite these benefits, AI adoption raises concerns. Algorithmic bias can disadvantage certain customer segments if training data is not carefully curated. Over-reliance on automated decisions may reduce human oversight in sensitive cases, while data privacy remains a persistent challenge as more customer information is processed.

Going forward, banks must adopt AI responsibly — ensuring transparency, regular algorithm audits, and robust data protection. Deployed thoughtfully, AI can make banking more efficient, inclusive and secure for millions of customers across India.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Climate finance has become a critical pillar of India's strategy to meet its net-zero emissions target by 2070. Banks and financial institutions are increasingly directing credit towards renewable energy projects, electric vehicle infrastructure, and sustainable agriculture, often supported by concessional lending rates and government-backed guarantee schemes. The Reserve Bank of India has encouraged banks to factor climate-related financial risks into their lending decisions, recognising that extreme weather events can affect borrowers' ability to repay loans, particularly in agriculture-dependent regions. Green bonds, which raise funds specifically for environmentally beneficial projects, have gained popularity among public sector banks and private corporations seeking to diversify funding sources while demonstrating environmental commitment. Despite this progress, experts note that climate finance in India remains a fraction of what is required to meet stated targets, and call for clearer regulatory frameworks, standardised green taxonomies, and greater private sector participation to bridge the funding gap."

(a) What is India's stated net-zero emissions target year? (2 marks)
(b) Name two sectors towards which banks are increasingly directing credit. (2 marks)
(c) Why has the RBI encouraged banks to factor climate-related risks into lending decisions? (2 marks)
(d) What is the purpose of green bonds, as described in the passage? (2 marks)
(e) According to experts, what is needed to bridge India's climate finance gap? (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) 2070.
(b) Renewable energy projects and electric vehicle infrastructure (or sustainable agriculture).
(c) Because extreme weather events can affect borrowers' ability to repay loans, particularly in agriculture-dependent regions.
(d) Green bonds raise funds specifically for environmentally beneficial projects, helping issuers diversify funding sources while showing environmental commitment.
(e) Clearer regulatory frameworks, standardised green taxonomies, and greater private sector participation.

questions.text: You are a Branch Manager at SBI. A customer's cheque for Rs 85,000 has bounced due to insufficient funds. Write a formal email to the customer informing them of the dishonour and the applicable charges as per bank policy.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Customer,

This is to inform you that your cheque no. 003421 for Rs 85,000 dated 12th June 2026 has been returned unpaid due to insufficient funds in your account.

As per bank policy, a cheque bounce charge of Rs 590 (plus applicable GST) has been debited to your account. Please note that repeated dishonour of cheques may affect your credit history and could attract penal action under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act.

We request you to maintain sufficient balance before issuing cheques in future and ensure the payee is informed accordingly.

For any clarification, please contact our branch at 1800-11-2211.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Branch Manager, SBI

questions.text: You are a PO at SBI handling SME accounts. A corporate client, M/s Sharma Textiles, has been waiting two weeks for disbursement of an approved working capital loan due to pending verification of collateral documents. Write an email explaining the delay and the documents still required.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Sir/Madam,

With reference to your approved working capital loan of Rs 25 lakh, we regret the delay in disbursement, which is due to pending verification of your collateral documents.

Our records show that the registered valuation report and the latest property tax receipt for the mortgaged premises are still awaited from your end. Once received, disbursement will be processed within 3 working days.

We value your business relationship and apologise for the inconvenience caused. Please submit the pending documents at your earliest to avoid further delay.

For assistance, contact your Relationship Manager directly.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: A customer's ATM card was retained by the machine during a failed transaction at your branch's ATM kiosk. Write an email to the customer explaining the reason, the retrieval process, and the expected timeline.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Mr./Ms. [Customer],

We understand your ATM card was retained by our machine at the Branch ATM kiosk during a failed transaction on 15th June 2026. This typically occurs due to a network timeout during card processing.

Your card has been safely captured and deposited with our branch. You may collect it from the branch counter along with a valid photo ID between 10 AM and 4 PM on any working day.

Alternatively, if you wish, we can arrange to send you a replacement card by post within 5-7 working days.

We apologise for the inconvenience caused.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: You are a PO at SBI. A customer has not paid the annual locker rent due for the last two months despite a reminder. Write an email to the customer requesting payment within 15 days and explaining the consequence of continued non-payment as per bank policy.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Customer,

This is a reminder that your annual locker rent of Rs 3,500 for Locker No. 214 has remained unpaid despite our earlier notice dated 1st June 2026.

We request you to clear the outstanding dues within 15 days of this email. Please note that as per bank policy, continued non-payment of locker rent beyond the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of locker access and, if unresolved, the bank may proceed with the breaking open of the locker as per due process.

We value you as our customer and request your immediate attention to avoid inconvenience.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: RBI has issued a revised circular on periodic KYC updation requirements for all bank branches, effective from next month. Write an email to all branch staff explaining the key changes and the action required from them.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Team,

Please note that RBI has issued a revised circular on periodic KYC updation, effective 1st July 2026. Key changes include a reduced re-KYC cycle for high-risk customers (from 2 years to 1 year) and mandatory digital KYC verification through Aadhaar-based e-KYC wherever possible.

All staff handling account opening and KYC updation must complete the online training module on our learning portal by 25th June. Branch heads are requested to identify customers due for KYC updation this quarter and initiate outreach accordingly.

For any queries, please reach out to the Compliance desk.

Regards,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: A customer's credit card application has been rejected due to a low CIBIL score. Write an email to the applicant explaining the reason for rejection and the steps they can take to improve eligibility for future applications.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Mr./Ms. [Applicant],

With reference to your recent credit card application, we regret to inform you that we are unable to approve it at this time, as your current CIBIL score does not meet our minimum eligibility criteria.

A low credit score is often the result of high existing credit utilisation, delayed EMI payments, or limited credit history. We encourage you to review your credit report, clear any outstanding dues, and maintain timely repayments on existing loans.

You are welcome to reapply after six months once your score improves. Our staff would be happy to guide you on building a healthier credit profile.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: Due to a system error, an incorrect (higher) interest amount was credited to a customer's Fixed Deposit account. Write an email to the customer explaining the error, the correction process, and apologising for the inconvenience.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Customer,

We wish to inform you of a system error that resulted in an incorrect, higher interest amount being credited to your Fixed Deposit account (No. XXXX5678) on 10th June 2026.

The excess amount of Rs 1,240 will be reversed from your linked savings account on 20th June 2026, restoring your FD interest to the correct, contracted rate. We sincerely apologise for this error and any confusion it may have caused.

Should you have already utilised these funds, please contact our branch to discuss a convenient repayment arrangement.

We regret the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: You are a PO at a busy SBI branch. With the festive season approaching, you expect a sharp rise in account opening and locker requests. Write an email to your Regional Manager requesting temporary additional staff for the next four weeks.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Sir/Madam,

With the festive season approaching, our branch anticipates a significant rise in footfall for new account openings, locker allotments, and gold loan enquiries over the next four weeks.

Our current staff strength is already stretched managing daily operations, and we are concerned about maintaining service quality during this peak period. I request the temporary deployment of two additional staff members, preferably with customer service experience, for the duration of the festive rush.

This will help us manage increased demand without compromising turnaround time or customer experience.

Kindly consider this request at the earliest.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: An NRI customer based in Dubai has written to your branch asking how to update his registered mobile number without visiting India in person. Write a reply email explaining the available options and required documents.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Mr. [Customer],

Thank you for reaching out regarding the update of your registered mobile number.

As you are unable to visit the branch in person, you may update your mobile number through our YONO SBI mobile application using Aadhaar-based e-KYC, provided your Aadhaar is linked to your account. Alternatively, you may send a duly signed request letter along with a self-attested copy of your passport and OCI/PIO card (if applicable) through registered post or courier to our branch.

Once received, the update will be processed within 3-5 working days, and you will receive a confirmation SMS and email.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: You are a PO at SBI. Write an email to a customer informing them that their Fixed Deposit is maturing in 7 days and asking for their instructions on renewal or closure.
questions.modelAnswer:
Dear Customer,

This is to inform you that your Fixed Deposit (Account No. XXXX9012) of Rs 2,00,000 is due to mature on 27th June 2026.

Kindly let us know your preference regarding this deposit: (i) renewal for a further term at the prevailing interest rate, (ii) renewal of principal with interest credited to your savings account, or (iii) closure with full proceeds transferred to your linked account.

If we do not receive your instructions before the maturity date, the deposit will be auto-renewed for the same tenure as per standing bank policy.

Please revert at your earliest convenience.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: You are a PO at SBI. A customer disputes an international transaction of $450 on his debit card that he claims he never made, while travelling abroad his card details may have been compromised. Describe the steps you will take immediately.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would first calm the customer and assure him the matter will be investigated promptly. I would immediately block his debit card to prevent further unauthorised use and help him raise a formal dispute/chargeback request with the date, amount and merchant details of the transaction.

I would guide him to file a written complaint at the branch and lodge a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in if fraud is suspected. I would explain the RBI Zero Liability policy and the typical 7-10 working day resolution timeline. Finally, I would advise him to monitor his account closely and enable transaction alerts.

questions.text: Two customers get into a heated argument in the branch lobby over queue jumping, disturbing other customers waiting for service. As the PO on duty, describe how you would handle the situation.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would calmly approach both customers and politely request them to lower their voices, assuring them I would resolve the matter fairly. I would check the token system to verify the correct order of service and explain it transparently to both parties.

If one customer had a genuine urgent need, I would offer to assist them separately without disrupting the queue. I would thank both customers for their patience, apologise for any inconvenience, and ensure the queue management system is functioning properly to prevent recurrence.

questions.text: A long-standing customer visits your branch upset about poor service and says she wants to close her account and move to another bank. As the PO, describe how you would handle this situation.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would listen patiently to her concerns without interrupting, to understand the exact reasons behind her dissatisfaction. I would sincerely apologise for the inconvenience caused and assure her that her feedback will be taken seriously.

I would offer to personally resolve any pending issues, such as service charges or transaction disputes, and inform her of recent improvements like extended service hours or digital banking options. If she still wishes to close her account, I would process the closure respectfully without pressuring her, while leaving the door open for her to return in future.

questions.text: An elderly, illiterate customer visits your branch to open a savings account but is unable to read or fill the application form. As the PO, describe how you would assist her.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would seat her comfortably and patiently explain the account opening process in simple language. I would read out each field of the form to her, fill it in on her behalf based on her responses, and explain the purpose of each document being collected, such as Aadhaar and PAN.

Before taking her thumb impression, I would read the entire form back to her clearly so she fully understands what she is agreeing to. I would also explain how to use her passbook and ATM card safely, and caution her against sharing her PIN or OTP with anyone.

questions.text: The core banking system goes down during peak banking hours, and a long queue of frustrated customers is waiting at your branch. As the PO on duty, describe how you would manage the situation.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would remain calm and immediately inform customers about the technical issue and the expected resolution time, to reduce uncertainty and frustration. I would request the IT helpdesk to escalate the issue urgently.

While the system is down, I would offer to process simple cash transactions manually where possible and provide acknowledgment slips. I would arrange seating and drinking water for waiting customers and keep them updated periodically. Once the system is restored, I would prioritise customers who have been waiting longest and apologise for the inconvenience caused.

questions.text: A customer approaches you for a personal loan but does not meet the bank's minimum eligibility criteria due to a low and irregular income. As the PO, describe how you would handle this situation.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would politely explain to the customer the specific eligibility criteria he does not currently meet, such as minimum stable income requirements, in a respectful and non-judgmental manner. I would avoid raising false hopes about loan approval.

I would explore alternative options with him, such as a smaller loan amount, a co-applicant with stable income, or a gold loan if he has suitable collateral. I would also suggest he build a stronger banking relationship through regular savings, which could improve his eligibility in future. I would thank him for his interest and encourage him to stay engaged with the bank.

questions.text: A senior citizen customer is unable to use the UPI app on her phone and is anxious about how she will make digital payments at her grandson's wedding shopping. As the PO, describe how you would assist her.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would patiently explain that she can continue using her debit card or cash for purchases if UPI feels unfamiliar, so she does not feel pressured. I would then offer to demonstrate the UPI app step by step, using a sample transaction, explaining concepts like UPI PIN and QR codes in simple terms.

I would write down the steps for her in simple language to take home for reference and caution her to never share her UPI PIN or OTP with anyone, including people claiming to be bank staff. I would also share our toll-free helpline number for any future difficulty.

questions.text: A customer reports that she withdrew Rs 2,000 from an ATM but received two fake/counterfeit notes among the cash dispensed. As the PO, describe the steps you will take.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would ask the customer to provide her ATM transaction slip, the approximate time of withdrawal, and the suspected fake notes for verification. I would check the ATM's CCTV footage and cash replenishment records for that specific cassette to verify her claim.

If the claim is substantiated, I would arrange for the fake notes to be impounded as per RBI guidelines and issue her a receipt, while crediting her account with the equivalent genuine amount after due verification. I would also flag the ATM for immediate cash audit to prevent further incidents and report the matter to the currency chest.

questions.text: The branch security guard reports that he stopped a suspicious individual attempting to access the strong room area without authorisation. As the PO on duty, describe the immediate steps you would take.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would first ensure the safety of staff and customers by calmly directing the security guard to detain the individual at the entrance without use of force, while I verify the situation. I would check CCTV footage to understand how the individual gained access to that area.

I would inform the Branch Manager immediately and, depending on the severity, contact the local police to hand over the individual for further investigation. I would also file an internal incident report and recommend a review of strong room access protocols to prevent recurrence.

questions.text: A pensioner customer visits your branch in distress, saying his monthly pension has not been credited and he urgently needs funds for medical expenses. As the PO, describe how you would handle this situation.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would empathise with the customer's urgency and immediately check his account and the pension credit batch records to identify the cause of delay, which is often due to a pending life certificate or a processing delay at the treasury end.

If the life certificate is the issue, I would help him complete it on the spot using the Jeevan Pramaan facility. I would escalate the matter to our pension processing cell for urgent resolution and, depending on bank policy, explore whether a temporary overdraft or emergency cash assistance can be extended given the medical urgency. I would keep him updated until resolved.

questions.text: You organised a Digital Banking Literacy Camp at a government degree college on 8th June 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager covering objectives, activities, attendance and outcomes.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON DIGITAL BANKING LITERACY CAMP

Date: 9th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Digital Banking Literacy Camp at Govt. Degree College — 8th June 2026

A Digital Banking Literacy Camp was organised at the college auditorium on 8th June 2026 to educate students about UPI, mobile banking, and online fraud prevention.

220 students attended the session. Live demonstrations of the YONO app and UPI transactions were conducted, along with a Q&A session on common digital frauds.

65 students opened new savings accounts on the spot, and 40 downloaded the YONO app during the camp.

Recommendation: Conduct similar camps each semester and partner with the college for a student banking cell.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: Your branch conducted an annual customer satisfaction survey covering 200 customers in May 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager summarising the methodology, key findings and recommendations.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON ANNUAL CUSTOMER SATISFACTION SURVEY

Date: 1st June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Customer Satisfaction Survey — May 2026

A structured survey covering 200 customers was conducted through in-branch questionnaires and phone calls during May 2026, assessing service quality, wait times and digital banking experience.

78% of customers rated overall service as good or excellent. However, 35% reported dissatisfaction with average waiting time during peak hours, and 20% were unaware of mobile banking features.

Recommendation: Deploy an additional counter during peak hours (11 AM-1 PM) and conduct monthly digital banking awareness sessions for walk-in customers.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: During an internal audit, a cash shortage of Rs 4,500 was detected at one of your branch's teller counters. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager detailing the findings and recommended corrective action.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON CASH SHORTAGE — TELLER COUNTER AUDIT

Date: 12th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Cash Shortage of Rs 4,500 — Internal Audit Findings

During the routine internal cash audit conducted on 11th June 2026, a shortage of Rs 4,500 was identified at Teller Counter 2. Preliminary review of transaction logs and CCTV footage suggests a probable counting error during a high cash-volume period rather than misappropriation.

The concerned teller has been asked to submit a written explanation, and cash counting has been temporarily moved to dual-verification mode at this counter.

Recommendation: Conduct refresher training on cash handling protocols and consider installing cash-counting machines at all counters.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: Your branch organised a blood donation camp in partnership with the district hospital on 14th June 2026 as part of its CSR initiative. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON BLOOD DONATION CAMP

Date: 15th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: CSR Blood Donation Camp — 14th June 2026

A blood donation camp was organised at the branch premises in partnership with the District Hospital on 14th June 2026, as part of the bank's CSR initiative.

68 units of blood were collected from staff, customers and local residents. The District Hospital's medical team conducted all health screenings and donations under proper safety protocols.

The event received positive feedback from the local community and was covered by a local newspaper.

Recommendation: Organise such camps quarterly and explore partnerships with nearby colleges to widen participation.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: Your branch recently inaugurated a new ATM kiosk in a remote village to improve banking access. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager covering the inauguration event and expected impact.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON INAUGURATION OF NEW ATM KIOSK

Date: 7th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Inauguration of ATM Kiosk at Village Devipur — 6th June 2026

A new ATM kiosk was inaugurated at Village Devipur on 6th June 2026 by the Regional Manager, in the presence of the Gram Panchayat head and local residents.

The kiosk is expected to serve approximately 1,500 households who previously had to travel 8 km to access banking services. Security measures including CCTV and a guard have been arranged.

Initial response from villagers was enthusiastic, with several enquiring about cash withdrawal and balance enquiry features.

Recommendation: Conduct a follow-up awareness camp on ATM usage and PIN safety for first-time users.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: Your branch conducted a mandatory staff training program on cybersecurity awareness on 10th June 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager summarising the training and outcomes.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON CYBERSECURITY AWARENESS TRAINING

Date: 11th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Staff Training on Cybersecurity Awareness — 10th June 2026

A half-day training session on cybersecurity awareness was conducted for all branch staff on 10th June 2026 by the Zonal IT Security team, covering phishing identification, safe password practices, and customer data handling protocols.

All 18 staff members attended and completed a post-training assessment, with an average score of 84%.

The session highlighted gaps in staff awareness of social engineering tactics used by fraudsters targeting bank employees.

Recommendation: Conduct quarterly refresher sessions and circulate monthly cybersecurity bulletins to reinforce best practices.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: Your branch organised a special grievance redressal camp exclusively for senior citizen customers on 5th June 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON SENIOR CITIZEN GRIEVANCE REDRESSAL CAMP

Date: 6th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Grievance Redressal Camp for Senior Citizens — 5th June 2026

A dedicated grievance redressal camp for senior citizen customers was organised at the branch on 5th June 2026, offering priority service for pension issues, KYC updation, and digital banking assistance.

95 senior citizens attended. 30 pension-related queries were resolved on the spot, and 45 customers were assisted with mobile banking registration.

Feedback collected was overwhelmingly positive, with many customers appreciating the dedicated time and patience shown by staff.

Recommendation: Institutionalise a fortnightly senior citizen service desk at the branch.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: Your branch organised a Loan Mela offering pre-approved personal, home and vehicle loans on 1st June 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager on the event's outcomes.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON LOAN MELA

Date: 2nd June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Loan Mela Outcomes — 1st June 2026

A Loan Mela was organised at the branch on 1st June 2026, offering on-the-spot processing for pre-approved personal, home and vehicle loans, with relaxed processing fees as a festive offer.

310 customers visited, of which 85 loan applications were logged on the same day, amounting to a sanctioned value of approximately Rs 4.2 crore. Home loans saw the highest interest, followed by vehicle loans.

The event significantly boosted branch loan disbursement targets for the month.

Recommendation: Conduct similar melas quarterly with targeted marketing in residential localities.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: Your branch has completed one year of full digital transition for account opening and KYC processes. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager assessing the impact on customer service.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON ONE YEAR OF DIGITAL TRANSITION

Date: 3rd June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Impact Assessment — One Year of Digital Account Opening

It has been one year since the branch fully transitioned to digital account opening and e-KYC processes. Average account opening time has reduced from 45 minutes to 12 minutes, and paper consumption has dropped by an estimated 70%.

Customer feedback has been largely positive, though 15% of elderly customers still prefer assisted, in-person processing.

Digital adoption has freed up staff time for higher-value customer interactions such as loan counselling and investment advisory.

Recommendation: Retain a dedicated assisted-service counter for customers less comfortable with digital processes.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text: Your branch conducted a mandatory fire safety drill on 9th June 2026 as per regulatory requirements. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager on the conduct and findings of the drill.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON FIRE SAFETY DRILL

Date: 10th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Fire Safety Drill Conducted — 9th June 2026

A mandatory fire safety drill was conducted at the branch on 9th June 2026 in coordination with the local fire department, covering evacuation procedures, fire extinguisher usage, and assembly point protocols.

All staff and customers present evacuated the premises within 4 minutes, meeting the required safety benchmark. Two fire extinguishers were found to be past their service date and have since been replaced.

Staff demonstrated good awareness of evacuation routes, though some were unfamiliar with extinguisher operation.

Recommendation: Conduct hands-on fire extinguisher training annually alongside the evacuation drill.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"Cybersecurity has become a critical concern for the banking sector as digital transactions continue to surge. Banks now face increasingly sophisticated threats, ranging from phishing attacks to ransomware targeting core banking systems. A single successful breach can compromise millions of customer records and erode public trust built over decades. Regulators worldwide are responding by mandating stricter data protection standards, regular security audits, and rapid breach disclosure timelines. However, technology alone cannot solve this challenge. Employee awareness, customer education on safe practices, and a culture of vigilance throughout the organisation are equally essential to building genuine cyber resilience."
questions.modelAnswer: Cybersecurity is a growing concern in banking as digital transactions rise. Banks face threats like phishing and ransomware, with breaches risking millions of records and eroding trust. Regulators are enforcing stricter data protection, audits, and disclosure norms. Yet technology alone is insufficient; employee awareness, customer education, and organisational vigilance are equally vital for genuine cyber resilience.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"Microfinance institutions have played a transformative role in extending credit to low-income households who lack access to traditional banking collateral. By offering small, unsecured loans through group-lending models, these institutions have enabled millions of rural entrepreneurs, particularly women, to start small businesses and improve household income. However, the sector has faced criticism over high interest rates and aggressive recovery practices in some cases, leading to borrower distress. Regulatory reforms in recent years have sought to cap interest rates and enforce responsible lending standards, aiming to balance financial inclusion with borrower protection."
questions.modelAnswer: Microfinance institutions have transformed credit access for low-income households lacking collateral, using group-lending models to help rural entrepreneurs, especially women, start businesses. However, high interest rates and aggressive recovery practices have drawn criticism, causing borrower distress in some cases. Recent regulatory reforms cap interest rates and enforce responsible lending, balancing financial inclusion with borrower protection.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"The gig economy has expanded rapidly in India, with millions now earning through platform-based work such as ride-hailing, food delivery and freelance services. While this offers flexibility and income opportunities, gig workers typically lack access to formal employment benefits such as provident fund, health insurance, and paid leave. Their irregular income patterns also make it difficult to access traditional credit and savings products designed for salaried employees. Financial institutions and policymakers are now exploring tailored products, including flexible savings schemes and portable social security benefits, to better serve this growing workforce."
questions.modelAnswer: India's gig economy has grown rapidly through platform-based work like ride-hailing and delivery, offering flexibility but lacking formal benefits such as provident fund and health insurance. Irregular incomes also limit gig workers' access to traditional credit and savings products. Financial institutions and policymakers are now developing tailored products, including flexible savings and portable social security, to serve this expanding workforce.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"India's renewable energy sector has attracted significant investment in recent years, driven by ambitious government targets and falling solar panel costs. Banks and financial institutions have increasingly extended credit to solar and wind energy projects, often supported by concessional financing schemes. Despite this progress, project developers continue to face challenges including delayed land acquisition, grid connectivity issues, and inconsistent state-level policy support. Industry experts argue that streamlining regulatory clearances and ensuring timely payments from power distribution companies are essential to sustaining the sector's growth momentum."
questions.modelAnswer: India's renewable energy sector has drawn significant investment due to government targets and falling solar costs, with banks extending credit through concessional financing. However, developers face challenges like delayed land acquisition, grid connectivity issues, and inconsistent state policies. Experts stress that streamlining regulatory clearances and ensuring timely payments from power distribution companies are essential for sustaining growth.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"Artificial intelligence is increasingly being deployed in banking customer service, with chatbots and virtual assistants handling a growing share of routine queries. This has reduced wait times and operational costs while allowing human staff to focus on complex issues requiring empathy and judgment. However, concerns remain about AI systems misunderstanding nuanced customer grievances or providing impersonal responses to sensitive matters such as fraud or financial distress. Banks are therefore adopting hybrid models, where AI handles routine queries but seamlessly escalates complex or emotionally sensitive cases to trained human representatives."
questions.modelAnswer: AI is increasingly used in banking customer service through chatbots handling routine queries, reducing wait times and costs while freeing staff for complex issues. However, concerns persist about AI misunderstanding nuanced grievances or responding impersonally to sensitive matters like fraud. Banks are adopting hybrid models where AI manages routine queries but escalates complex or sensitive cases to trained human staff.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"Self-help groups have emerged as a powerful vehicle for women's financial empowerment in rural India, enabling members to pool savings and access collective credit without traditional collateral. Beyond financial inclusion, these groups have fostered leadership skills, social cohesion, and greater participation of women in household and community decision-making. Banks have increasingly partnered with SHGs through linkage programmes, extending credit at favourable terms based on the group's savings track record. However, sustaining group cohesion and ensuring equitable benefit distribution among members remain ongoing challenges for many SHG networks."
questions.modelAnswer: Self-help groups have empowered rural Indian women financially by enabling pooled savings and collective credit access without collateral, while also building leadership skills and boosting women's participation in decision-making. Banks have partnered with SHGs through linkage programmes offering favourable credit terms based on savings history. However, sustaining group cohesion and ensuring equitable benefit distribution remain ongoing challenges.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"Managing inflation remains one of the Reserve Bank of India's primary mandates, achieved largely through adjustments to the repo rate, the rate at which it lends to commercial banks. Raising the repo rate makes borrowing costlier, typically cooling consumer spending and investment, thereby moderating inflation. However, this approach also risks slowing economic growth and increasing EMI burdens on existing borrowers. The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee must therefore carefully balance inflation control against growth objectives, basing decisions on a wide range of economic indicators including food prices, global crude oil trends, and currency stability."
questions.modelAnswer: The RBI manages inflation primarily by adjusting the repo rate, with rate hikes raising borrowing costs to cool spending and moderate inflation. However, this risks slowing growth and increasing EMI burdens on borrowers. The Monetary Policy Committee must balance inflation control against growth, considering indicators such as food prices, global crude trends, and currency stability.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"India's start-up ecosystem has matured significantly over the past decade, supported by a combination of venture capital funding, government initiatives like Start-up India, and an expanding pool of skilled talent. Banks have traditionally been cautious lenders to early-stage start-ups due to the absence of collateral and unproven business models. However, specialised start-up financing schemes, venture debt products, and credit guarantee mechanisms are gradually emerging to bridge this gap. Industry observers note that deeper bank participation could significantly reduce start-ups' dependence on equity dilution for early-stage capital needs."
questions.modelAnswer: India's start-up ecosystem has matured through venture capital, government initiatives like Start-up India, and growing skilled talent. Banks have traditionally been cautious lenders to early-stage start-ups lacking collateral or proven models, but specialised financing schemes, venture debt, and credit guarantees are emerging to bridge this gap. Greater bank participation could reduce start-ups' reliance on equity dilution for early capital needs.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"Digital literacy among elderly citizens remains a significant barrier to full financial inclusion in India, even as banking services increasingly shift online. Many elderly customers, despite holding bank accounts, remain dependent on family members or bank staff for basic digital transactions, making them vulnerable to both exclusion and fraud. Banks have begun addressing this through simplified app interfaces, dedicated senior citizen helplines, and in-branch digital literacy desks. Experts argue that sustained, patient, in-person training, rather than one-time awareness camps, is key to building lasting digital confidence among this demographic."
questions.modelAnswer: Digital literacy among elderly citizens remains a barrier to financial inclusion despite the shift to online banking, leaving many dependent on family or staff and vulnerable to exclusion and fraud. Banks are addressing this through simplified app interfaces, senior citizen helplines, and in-branch digital literacy desks. Experts argue sustained, patient, in-person training, not one-time camps, builds lasting digital confidence.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in approximately 80-90 words.

"Non-performing assets have long posed a challenge to the health of India's banking sector, tying up capital that could otherwise support fresh lending. In recent years, the introduction of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code has provided a faster, more structured route for resolving stressed assets compared to earlier mechanisms. Banks have also strengthened early-warning systems to flag stressed accounts before they turn into full defaults. While gross NPA ratios have improved meaningfully from their peak, experts caution that asset quality must be watched closely as interest rates and economic cycles fluctuate."
questions.modelAnswer: Non-performing assets have long challenged Indian banking by tying up capital needed for fresh lending. The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code has enabled faster, more structured resolution of stressed assets than earlier mechanisms, while banks have strengthened early-warning systems to flag defaults early. Although gross NPA ratios have improved from their peak, experts caution that asset quality needs close monitoring amid fluctuating rates and economic cycles.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Cashless Economy: Opportunities and Challenges for India."
questions.modelAnswer:
India has made significant strides towards becoming a cashless economy, driven by the rapid adoption of UPI, mobile wallets, and government initiatives promoting digital payments. This shift offers substantial benefits, including reduced costs of printing and handling currency, improved transparency that curbs black money and tax evasion, and greater convenience for consumers and businesses alike.

For small vendors and informal businesses, digital payments have opened access to formal credit by creating a verifiable transaction history, something previously unavailable to cash-only enterprises. Government welfare transfers have also become faster and more transparent through direct digital disbursement.

However, the transition is not without challenges. A significant section of the population, particularly in rural and remote areas, still lacks reliable internet connectivity and digital literacy. Elderly and less tech-savvy citizens often struggle to adapt, risking financial exclusion if cash options are withdrawn too quickly. Cybersecurity threats, including phishing and payment fraud, have also risen alongside digital adoption, disproportionately affecting first-time users.

A fully cashless economy must therefore be approached gradually and inclusively. Continued investment in digital infrastructure, vernacular-language interfaces, and sustained financial literacy campaigns are essential to ensure no one is left behind. Cash should remain available as a fallback option, particularly for vulnerable populations, even as digital adoption deepens.

In conclusion, while a cashless economy promises efficiency, transparency and inclusion, its success ultimately depends on building trust, accessibility, and safety nets alongside the technology itself.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Role of Women in India's Banking Sector Growth."
questions.modelAnswer:
Women have played an increasingly significant role in shaping India's banking sector, both as employees and as customers driving financial inclusion. Today, women occupy leadership positions across public and private sector banks, bringing diverse perspectives to strategy, risk management, and customer service.

On the customer side, initiatives like the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana have brought millions of previously unbanked women into the formal financial system, often for the first time controlling their own savings independently. Self-help groups, frequently led by women, have further deepened financial inclusion in rural areas, fostering both economic independence and improved household decision-making power.

Banks have also recognised women as a distinct and valuable customer segment, designing tailored products such as women-focused savings accounts, lower-interest business loans for women entrepreneurs, and insurance schemes addressing specific life-stage needs. This targeted approach has helped narrow the historical gender gap in financial access.

Despite this progress, challenges remain. Women in rural areas often face social and logistical barriers to accessing banking services independently, and representation of women in senior banking leadership, while improving, remains below parity. Addressing these gaps requires continued investment in last-mile banking infrastructure, mentorship programmes for women employees, and culturally sensitive outreach for women customers.

In conclusion, women are no longer peripheral to banking sector growth but central to it, both as professionals shaping the industry and as customers whose financial empowerment strengthens the broader economy.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Cybersecurity Challenges in the Era of Digital Banking."
questions.modelAnswer:
As banking services increasingly shift to digital platforms, cybersecurity has emerged as one of the sector's most pressing challenges. The convenience of mobile and internet banking has been matched by a corresponding rise in sophisticated cyber threats, ranging from phishing and identity theft to large-scale ransomware attacks targeting core banking infrastructure.

Financial institutions are attractive targets due to the sheer volume of sensitive data and funds they manage. A single successful breach can compromise millions of customer records, cause significant financial loss, and severely damage public trust built over years. Smaller cooperative banks and regional players, often with limited cybersecurity budgets, are particularly vulnerable compared to larger institutions with dedicated security teams.

Customers themselves remain a critical vulnerability, as social engineering tactics like fake customer-care calls and phishing links continue to exploit limited digital literacy, particularly among first-time digital users and senior citizens. Regulators have responded with stricter mandates around data protection, multi-factor authentication, and rapid breach disclosure timelines, pushing banks to strengthen their defences.

Addressing this challenge requires a multi-layered approach: robust technical safeguards such as encryption and real-time fraud detection, continuous employee training to recognise emerging threats, and sustained customer education campaigns on safe digital practices. Collaboration between banks, regulators, and law enforcement is equally essential to track and disrupt organised cybercrime networks.

In conclusion, cybersecurity in digital banking is not a one-time fix but an ongoing commitment, requiring constant vigilance as both technology and threats continue to evolve.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Sustainable Development: The Role of Banks in a Greener Economy."
questions.modelAnswer:
As the urgency of addressing climate change grows, banks are increasingly recognised as key enablers of sustainable development, given their central role in allocating capital across the economy. Through green lending, banks can direct credit towards renewable energy, energy-efficient infrastructure, and environmentally responsible businesses, while curbing financing for highly polluting industries.

Many Indian banks have begun issuing green bonds to raise dedicated funding for environmentally beneficial projects, while also incorporating climate-related risks into their credit assessment frameworks. This recognises that extreme weather events and shifting regulations can directly affect borrowers' ability to repay loans, particularly in climate-sensitive sectors like agriculture.

Beyond lending, banks influence sustainability through their own operations, including reducing paper usage via digital banking, adopting energy-efficient branch infrastructure, and setting internal carbon reduction targets. Some institutions have also begun offering sustainability-linked loans, where interest rates are tied to a borrower's environmental performance, creating direct financial incentives for greener business practices.

However, challenges remain. The absence of standardised green taxonomies makes it difficult to consistently classify and compare environmentally beneficial projects across institutions, raising concerns about superficial or exaggerated sustainability claims. Building genuine expertise in assessing environmental risk also requires significant investment in staff training and data systems.

In conclusion, banks occupy a uniquely influential position in the transition to a sustainable economy. By embedding environmental considerations into core lending and operational decisions, the sector can meaningfully accelerate, rather than merely accommodate, India's progress towards its climate commitments.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Impact of Social Media on Banking and Customer Engagement."
questions.modelAnswer:
Social media has fundamentally transformed how banks engage with customers, shifting communication from one-way notices to interactive, real-time conversations. Platforms once used purely for marketing are now key channels for customer service, financial education, and even product launches, reflecting how deeply social media has embedded itself in banking strategy.

For customers, social media offers a convenient avenue to raise queries or complaints publicly, often prompting faster resolution than traditional channels due to reputational considerations. Banks have responded by setting up dedicated social media response teams to handle queries swiftly and professionally. Educational content, including short videos explaining fraud prevention or new digital features, has also proven effective in reaching younger, digitally native audiences.

However, this shift carries risks. Misinformation or rumours about a bank's financial health can spread rapidly on social media, potentially triggering unwarranted panic among customers. Sensitive customer complaints discussed publicly can also expose private financial details if not handled carefully. Additionally, fraudsters increasingly exploit social media to run fake customer-care accounts, deceiving unsuspecting customers into sharing confidential information.

Banks must therefore balance the engagement benefits of social media with robust risk management: verified official accounts, rapid rumour-correction protocols, and clear guidance to customers on identifying genuine bank communication. Staff handling these platforms also require specific training in both customer service and crisis communication.

In conclusion, social media has become an indispensable tool for modern banking engagement, but its effective use demands as much caution and governance as it does creativity and responsiveness.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Financial Literacy: The Need of the Hour."
questions.modelAnswer:
As financial products grow increasingly complex and digital banking becomes the norm, financial literacy has emerged as an essential life skill rather than a specialised one. Despite expanding access to bank accounts through initiatives like the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, many account holders remain unaware of how to use these accounts meaningfully for savings, credit, or insurance.

Low financial literacy has tangible consequences. Individuals without a basic understanding of interest rates, loan terms, or investment risk are more vulnerable to predatory lending, unsuitable insurance products, and financial fraud. The rapid rise of digital payment fraud, often targeting first-time digital users unfamiliar with safe practices, further underscores this gap.

Financial literacy also has a broader economic dimension. A population that understands budgeting, saving, and responsible borrowing contributes to greater household financial stability and, by extension, a more resilient economy less vulnerable to debt-driven crises. Women and rural populations, historically underserved in financial education, stand to benefit disproportionately from targeted literacy efforts.

Addressing this gap requires a coordinated, multi-pronged approach: integrating financial education into school curricula from an early age, leveraging Business Correspondents and self-help groups for grassroots outreach, and designing simplified, vernacular-language digital tools that make financial concepts more accessible. Banks themselves have a role to play through ongoing customer education, not merely product promotion.

In conclusion, financial literacy is no longer optional in an increasingly complex financial landscape. Sustained, inclusive investment in financial education is essential to ensuring that expanded access to banking truly translates into financial well-being for all.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Start-up India: The Role of Banks in Nurturing Entrepreneurship."
questions.modelAnswer:
India's start-up ecosystem has grown remarkably over the past decade, supported by government initiatives such as Start-up India, an expanding pool of skilled talent, and increasing venture capital interest. Within this ecosystem, banks occupy a uniquely important, if traditionally cautious, role in providing the credit infrastructure that early-stage ventures need to scale.

Historically, banks have been reluctant lenders to start-ups, given the absence of tangible collateral, unproven business models, and higher failure rates compared to established businesses. This has pushed many start-ups towards equity financing, often diluting founder ownership earlier than ideal. However, this dynamic is gradually shifting, with banks introducing venture debt products, collateral-free credit guarantee schemes, and specialised start-up banking verticals designed to better understand and serve this segment.

Beyond direct lending, banks support entrepreneurship through ancillary services such as current account solutions tailored to start-up cash-flow patterns, payment gateway integrations, and advisory support on financial structuring. Partnerships between banks and start-up incubators have also become increasingly common, providing founders with both mentorship and easier access to formal credit channels.

Challenges remain, however. Risk assessment models calibrated for traditional businesses often do not translate well to evaluating start-ups, where intangible assets like intellectual property or user base may matter more than physical collateral. Building genuine expertise in start-up risk evaluation requires sustained investment in specialised underwriting capabilities.

In conclusion, as India's start-up ecosystem matures, deeper and more sophisticated bank participation could meaningfully reduce founders' dependence on equity dilution, strengthening both individual ventures and the broader entrepreneurial economy.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Climate Change: A Growing Responsibility for the Banking Sector."
questions.modelAnswer:
Climate change is no longer a peripheral concern for banks but a central risk factor shaping lending decisions, regulatory expectations, and long-term institutional strategy. As extreme weather events grow more frequent, banks face direct exposure through borrowers in climate-vulnerable sectors such as agriculture, real estate, and infrastructure, whose ability to repay loans can be significantly disrupted by floods, droughts, or cyclones.

Recognising this, regulators including the Reserve Bank of India have begun encouraging banks to factor climate-related financial risks into their credit assessment frameworks, treating environmental risk with the same seriousness traditionally reserved for credit or market risk. Banks have responded by developing climate risk disclosure frameworks and stress-testing loan portfolios against various climate scenarios.

Beyond risk management, banks also bear a proactive responsibility to channel capital towards climate solutions. Green bonds, concessional financing for renewable energy projects, and sustainability-linked loans that reward environmentally responsible business practices are increasingly common tools through which banks can actively support, rather than merely accommodate, the transition to a low-carbon economy.

Challenges persist, however. The absence of standardised methodologies for measuring climate risk makes consistent assessment difficult, and smaller institutions often lack the technical expertise or data infrastructure to model these risks effectively. Balancing near-term commercial pressures against long-term climate responsibility also remains a genuine institutional tension.

In conclusion, climate change has fundamentally expanded what responsible banking means. Institutions that proactively integrate environmental considerations into both risk management and lending strategy will be better positioned for long-term resilience in an increasingly climate-constrained economy.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Work From Home Culture: Implications for the Banking Workforce."
questions.modelAnswer:
The widespread adoption of remote and hybrid work arrangements, accelerated significantly during the pandemic years, has prompted banks to reconsider long-standing assumptions about how banking work must be performed. While core branch operations inherently require physical presence, many back-office, technology, and even certain customer relationship functions have proven adaptable to remote arrangements.

For employees, hybrid work has offered greater flexibility, reduced commuting burdens, and improved work-life balance, factors increasingly important in attracting and retaining talent in a competitive job market. Banks that have embraced flexible arrangements report improved employee satisfaction and, in some cases, productivity gains in roles involving focused analytical or technical work.

However, banking carries unique challenges in this transition. Data security and customer confidentiality requirements demand robust remote-access protocols, secure devices, and strict monitoring, adding both cost and operational complexity. Collaborative, mentorship-heavy functions, particularly important for training junior staff, have also proven more difficult to replicate effectively in remote settings, raising concerns about long-term skill development and organisational culture.

Customer-facing roles present a further dilemma. While some routine interactions have moved successfully online, complex advisory conversations, particularly around loans or investments, often still benefit from in-person trust-building that remote channels struggle to fully replicate.

Given these trade-offs, most banks appear to be converging on hybrid models, combining the flexibility benefits of remote work with periodic in-person collaboration and training. In conclusion, work-from-home culture is reshaping the banking workforce, not as a wholesale replacement for traditional structures but as a complementary mode requiring thoughtful, function-specific implementation.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Cryptocurrency and the Future of Indian Banking."
questions.modelAnswer:
The rise of cryptocurrency has presented both a challenge and a catalyst for innovation within India's traditionally conservative banking sector. While private cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin remain outside formal regulatory endorsement in India, their growing global adoption has pushed policymakers and banks to seriously engage with the underlying blockchain technology and its implications for the future of money.

One significant response has been the Reserve Bank of India's introduction of the Central Bank Digital Currency, or digital rupee, which seeks to combine the efficiency benefits of digital currency with the stability and regulatory oversight of traditional fiat money. Unlike private cryptocurrencies, the digital rupee is fully backed and regulated by the central bank, addressing key concerns around volatility and consumer protection.

For banks, cryptocurrency-adjacent technologies also offer potential efficiency gains beyond currency itself, including blockchain-based trade finance settlement, faster cross-border payments, and improved transaction transparency. Some banks have begun piloting blockchain applications in these areas, even while remaining cautious about private cryptocurrency exposure.

However, significant concerns persist. The volatility and speculative nature of private cryptocurrencies raise consumer protection issues, while their potential misuse for money laundering and illicit transactions has drawn close regulatory scrutiny worldwide. Indian regulators have consequently adopted a cautious, calibrated approach, prioritising the regulated digital rupee over endorsing private cryptocurrency adoption.

In conclusion, while private cryptocurrencies remain a contested space, the broader technological shift they represent is meaningfully influencing Indian banking, most notably through the digital rupee, signalling a measured rather than disruptive path towards the future of digital money.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Microfinance institutions have played a transformative role in extending credit to low-income households that lack access to traditional banking collateral. By offering small, unsecured loans through group-lending models, these institutions have enabled millions of rural entrepreneurs, particularly women, to start small businesses. However, the sector has faced criticism over high interest rates and aggressive recovery practices in some cases. Regulatory reforms in recent years have sought to cap interest rates and enforce responsible lending standards, balancing financial inclusion with borrower protection."

(a) How do microfinance institutions extend credit to low-income households lacking collateral? (2 marks)
(b) Who has particularly benefited from these group-lending models, according to the passage? (2 marks)
(c) What criticism has the microfinance sector faced? (2 marks)
(d) What has been the aim of recent regulatory reforms in this sector? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "changed completely for the better." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) They extend credit through small, unsecured loans using group-lending models.
(b) Rural entrepreneurs, particularly women, have benefited the most.
(c) The sector has been criticised for high interest rates and aggressive recovery practices in some cases.
(d) Recent reforms aim to cap interest rates and enforce responsible lending standards.
(e) Transformative.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Managing inflation remains one of the Reserve Bank of India's primary mandates, achieved largely through adjustments to the repo rate, the rate at which it lends to commercial banks. Raising the repo rate makes borrowing costlier, typically cooling consumer spending and moderating inflation. However, this approach risks slowing economic growth and increasing EMI burdens on existing borrowers. The Monetary Policy Committee must therefore carefully balance inflation control against growth objectives, basing decisions on indicators including food prices, global crude oil trends, and currency stability."

(a) What is the repo rate, as defined in the passage? (2 marks)
(b) How does raising the repo rate help control inflation? (2 marks)
(c) What is one risk associated with raising the repo rate? (2 marks)
(d) Name two indicators the Monetary Policy Committee considers in its decisions. (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "directive" or "official task." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends to commercial banks.
(b) It makes borrowing costlier, which cools consumer spending and moderates inflation.
(c) It risks slowing economic growth and increasing EMI burdens on existing borrowers.
(d) Food prices and global crude oil trends (or currency stability).
(e) Mandate.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"India's start-up ecosystem has matured significantly over the past decade, supported by venture capital funding, government initiatives like Start-up India, and an expanding pool of skilled talent. Banks have traditionally been cautious lenders to early-stage start-ups due to the absence of collateral and unproven business models. However, specialised start-up financing schemes, venture debt products, and credit guarantee mechanisms are gradually emerging to bridge this gap. Deeper bank participation could significantly reduce start-ups' dependence on equity dilution for early-stage capital needs."

(a) Name two factors that have supported the growth of India's start-up ecosystem. (2 marks)
(b) Why have banks traditionally been cautious lenders to early-stage start-ups? (2 marks)
(c) What new financing tools are emerging to address this gap? (2 marks)
(d) What benefit could deeper bank participation offer to start-ups, according to the passage? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "reduction in ownership share." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) Venture capital funding and government initiatives like Start-up India (or skilled talent).
(b) Due to the absence of collateral and unproven business models.
(c) Specialised start-up financing schemes, venture debt products, and credit guarantee mechanisms.
(d) It could reduce start-ups' dependence on equity dilution for early-stage capital.
(e) Dilution.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Cybersecurity has become a critical concern for banks as digital transactions surge. Threats range from phishing attacks to ransomware targeting core banking systems, and a single breach can compromise millions of customer records, eroding decades of public trust. Regulators worldwide are mandating stricter data protection standards, regular audits, and rapid breach disclosure timelines. However, technology alone cannot solve this challenge; employee awareness, customer education, and organisational vigilance are equally essential to building genuine cyber resilience."

(a) Why has cybersecurity become a critical concern for banks? (2 marks)
(b) What can a single successful breach lead to, according to the passage? (2 marks)
(c) Name two measures regulators are mandating in response. (2 marks)
(d) Besides technology, what else is essential for building cyber resilience? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "the ability to recover quickly from difficulties." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) Because digital transactions are surging, exposing banks to threats like phishing and ransomware.
(b) It can compromise millions of customer records and erode public trust.
(c) Stricter data protection standards and regular security audits (or rapid breach disclosure).
(d) Employee awareness, customer education, and organisational vigilance.
(e) Resilience.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Self-help groups have emerged as a powerful vehicle for women's financial empowerment in rural India, enabling members to pool savings and access collective credit without traditional collateral. Beyond financial inclusion, these groups have fostered leadership skills and greater participation of women in household decision-making. Banks have increasingly partnered with SHGs through linkage programmes, extending credit at favourable terms based on the group's savings track record. However, sustaining group cohesion and ensuring equitable benefit distribution remain ongoing challenges."

(a) How do self-help groups enable rural women to access credit without collateral? (2 marks)
(b) Apart from financial inclusion, what other benefits have SHGs fostered? (2 marks)
(c) On what basis do banks extend credit to SHGs through linkage programmes? (2 marks)
(d) What challenges does the passage mention regarding SHG networks? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "fair" or "equal." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) By pooling members' savings and enabling collective access to credit.
(b) Leadership skills and greater participation of women in household decision-making.
(c) Credit is extended based on the group's savings track record.
(d) Sustaining group cohesion and ensuring equitable benefit distribution among members.
(e) Equitable.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Non-performing assets have long posed a challenge to the health of India's banking sector, tying up capital that could otherwise support fresh lending.
