The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code has provided a faster, more structured route for resolving stressed assets compared to earlier mechanisms. Banks have also strengthened early-warning systems to flag stressed accounts before they turn into full defaults. While gross NPA ratios have improved meaningfully from their peak, experts caution that asset quality must be watched closely."

(a) Why are non-performing assets a challenge for banks, according to the passage? (2 marks)
(b) What has the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code enabled, as per the passage? (2 marks)
(c) How are banks trying to prevent accounts from turning into full defaults? (2 marks)
(d) What caution do experts express despite the improvement in NPA ratios? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "the highest point." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) Because they tie up capital that could otherwise support fresh lending.
(b) A faster, more structured route for resolving stressed assets compared to earlier mechanisms.
(c) By strengthening early-warning systems to flag stressed accounts early.
(d) That asset quality must be watched closely amid fluctuating rates and economic cycles.
(e) Peak.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Artificial intelligence is increasingly deployed in banking customer service, with chatbots handling a growing share of routine queries, reducing wait times and costs while freeing staff for complex issues. However, concerns remain about AI misunderstanding nuanced grievances or responding impersonally to sensitive matters such as fraud or financial distress. Banks are therefore adopting hybrid models, where AI handles routine queries but escalates complex or emotionally sensitive cases to trained human representatives."

(a) What benefits has AI brought to banking customer service, according to the passage? (2 marks)
(b) What concern does the passage raise about AI in customer service? (2 marks)
(c) What kind of issues might AI struggle to handle appropriately? (2 marks)
(d) What approach are banks adopting to address this concern? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "emotionally delicate" or "requiring careful handling." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) AI has reduced wait times and costs by handling routine queries, freeing staff for complex issues.
(b) That AI may misunderstand nuanced grievances or respond impersonally to sensitive matters.
(c) Sensitive matters such as fraud or financial distress.
(d) Banks are adopting hybrid models where AI handles routine queries but escalates sensitive cases to humans.
(e) Sensitive.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"India's gig economy has expanded rapidly, with millions earning through platform-based work such as ride-hailing and food delivery. While this offers flexibility and income opportunities, gig workers typically lack access to formal employment benefits such as provident fund and health insurance. Their irregular income patterns also make it difficult to access traditional credit products designed for salaried employees. Financial institutions are now exploring tailored products, including flexible savings schemes and portable social security benefits, to better serve this workforce."

(a) What examples of gig economy work does the passage mention? (2 marks)
(b) What formal benefits do gig workers typically lack, according to the passage? (2 marks)
(c) Why do gig workers find it difficult to access traditional credit products? (2 marks)
(d) What solutions are financial institutions exploring for gig workers? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "unable to be carried over" — actually meaning "can be carried or transferred." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) Ride-hailing and food delivery, among other platform-based work.
(b) Provident fund and health insurance.
(c) Because their irregular income patterns do not suit products designed for salaried employees.
(d) Flexible savings schemes and portable social security benefits.
(e) Portable.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Digital literacy among elderly citizens remains a significant barrier to full financial inclusion in India, even as banking services increasingly shift online. Many elderly customers remain dependent on family members or bank staff for basic digital transactions, making them vulnerable to both exclusion and fraud. Banks have begun addressing this through simplified app interfaces, dedicated senior citizen helplines, and in-branch digital literacy desks. Sustained, patient, in-person training, rather than one-time awareness camps, is key to building lasting digital confidence."

(a) What barrier do elderly citizens face in achieving full financial inclusion? (2 marks)
(b) What does this dependence on others make elderly customers vulnerable to? (2 marks)
(c) Name two steps banks have taken to address this issue. (2 marks)
(d) What does the passage suggest is key to building lasting digital confidence? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "easily harmed or exploited." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) Limited digital literacy, despite banking services shifting increasingly online.
(b) Exclusion and fraud.
(c) Simplified app interfaces and dedicated senior citizen helplines (or in-branch digital literacy desks).
(d) Sustained, patient, in-person training, rather than one-time awareness camps.
(e) Vulnerable.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Climate finance has become a critical pillar of India's strategy to meet its net-zero emissions target. Banks are increasingly directing credit towards renewable energy projects, electric vehicle infrastructure, and sustainable agriculture, often supported by concessional lending. The RBI has encouraged banks to factor climate-related risks into lending decisions, recognising that extreme weather can affect borrowers' repayment ability. Green bonds have gained popularity among banks seeking to diversify funding while demonstrating environmental commitment, though experts call for clearer regulatory frameworks to bridge the funding gap."

(a) Towards which sectors are banks increasingly directing credit, according to the passage? (2 marks)
(b) Why has the RBI encouraged banks to factor climate risks into lending decisions? (2 marks)
(c) What purpose do green bonds serve for banks, as per the passage? (2 marks)
(d) What do experts call for to bridge the climate finance funding gap? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage meaning "essential support" or "foundation." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) Renewable energy projects, electric vehicle infrastructure, and sustainable agriculture.
(b) Because extreme weather can affect borrowers' ability to repay loans.
(c) They help banks diversify funding sources while demonstrating environmental commitment.
(d) Clearer regulatory frameworks (and standardised green taxonomies).
(e) Pillar.

typingParas: Success comes from consistent effort, not sudden motivation. Every day offers a chance to improve skills, learn something new, and move closer to goals through patience, discipline, and persistence.

The Mains Examination covers various topics from reasoning and computer aptitude, English language, Data Analysis & Interpretation, and general/economy/banking awareness. Computer Aptitude is a part of the Reasoning section in the mains exam. The subject-wise topics that need to be covered for preparing for the SBI PO 2026 Mains Exam have been listed below.

SBI PO Descriptive Test is a part of the mains examination. It is based on two questions for 50 marks for 30 minutes. It is a Test of English Language (Letter Writing & Essay). Besides the preliminary exam and the main exam, an interview process is also conducted for candidates who qualify for the first two phases of the SBI PO exam. The final merit list is made based on marks obtained by a candidate in his/ her main exam and interview process.
